best: Strong reuse/leak review: two unclosed FileIO resource leaks, missing withPartition, and several correct nudges to reuse Iceberg ContentFileUtil helpers (referencedDataFileLocation, isFileScoped, isDV) and fileSizeInBytes that I would plausibly miss blind.

Mostly simplification/inlining nits on a Guava-to-CompletableFuture refactor; the only non-obvious point is suggesting a type parameter on the callback interface to drop the unchecked Iterator<Result> cast.

The beginDispatch/try-ordering catch is a genuinely non-obvious correctness point (endDispatch must not run if beginDispatch threw), plus the chained suggestion to pass null and let the EQE default handler apply rather than plumb a handler through.

Architectural depth: argues the scan config options don't belong in the Java API surface and that fluo-core should not depend on commons-csv (push it behind a Function passed from fluo-command), plus combined-option sanity checks.

Solid API-boundary review: flags leaking an internal runtime package and SchemaGeneratorConfigBuilder as public API, collapsing two useless CDI beans into ToolManagerImpl, @Dependent scoping, and using runtime dependencies instead of indexing.

Best catch is that the indicator-class-based failure analysis is broken (spring-webmvc/webflux present with no embedded server still triggers the missing-factory failure), backed by sound advice on the exception hierarchy, constructor message ownership, removing the unused cause constructor, and package tangle.

Asks the right semantic question about switching from Iceberg StructLikeWrapper hashing/comparison to Trino's internal hash/comparators, plus narrowing the exposed FlatHashStrategy SPI and flattening the AND filter tree.
The diff is empty (0 changed files), so none of the MetricsReporter API design points can be verified against the diff under the correctness criterion.

API-design depth on a new client surface: auth factory and transaction creation should be async because they involve IO, and protobuf Schema should bind to com.google.protobuf.Message rather than the generated GeneratedMessageV3.

Catches that the correlation visitor misses table-reference joins (a real correctness gap), and pushes a dedicated checker class with an exception-based early exit (and fillInStackTrace override) plus moving hasCorrelatedSubquery into AbstractSqlAstTranslator.

Subtle datetime-literal correctness: spotting the need to force an offset (append 'Z' when jdbcTimeZone is null), questioning the calendar transformation, and the UTC-offset fixes on the LocalTime/OffsetTime timestamp paths.
